audience: Business owners, marketers and communications leads at SMEs considering professional photography for their website, campaigns, recruitment or sales
primaryDutchSearchIntent: KMO's uitleggen wat bedrijfsfotografie omvat, welke beeldtypes bij verschillende doelen passen en hoe ze een bruikbare productie voorbereiden

searchIntent.question: What does corporate photography include, when does an SME need it and how do you plan a useful image library?
searchIntent.geographicContext: English-speaking SMEs in Limburg, Belgium and the surrounding Belgian market

directAnswer: Corporate photography is professional photography that shows who an organisation is, what it does and how it works. It can cover people, processes, products, locations, completed projects and events, all planned around specific uses such as a website, sales, recruitment and campaigns.
title: What is corporate photography and when does your SME need it?
metaDescription: What is corporate photography? Learn which images your SME needs and how to plan the shot list, usage rights, formats, selection and publication.
h1: What is corporate photography and when does your SME need it?

internalLinks.anchor: prepare the brief, shot list, location and schedule
create consistent and recognisable corporate portraits
why a fast website can rank better
local SEO for SMEs in Limburg
why invest in a corporate video

factsToPreserve: Corporate photography covers more than a group portrait or attractive building and may include people, processes, products, locations and completed projects
A well-prepared image library can provide value for several years but no fixed lifespan or commercial result is guaranteed
Original photography can support trust but photography alone does not guarantee sales
One production day can combine several image types only when planning and circumstances allow
Agreement to be photographed does not automatically mean agreement to every publication or distribution
File ownership, copyright and a usage licence are not automatically the same
The article gives practical production information and is not legal advice
Google recommends relevant context and useful alt text and warns against keyword stuffing in alt attributes
Meaningful non-text content requires text alternatives under WCAG 2.2
A photograph does not produce a local ranking by itself
Photography and video can be combined but require different direction, production flow and post-production
Price and lead time can include preparation, travel, location research, assistance, lighting, selection, editing, formats, licensing and archiving
